title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot discusses the murder of a child named Joyce with Roya Dreyfus (also referred to as Mrs. Drake/Dreyer/Dreck). Roya suggests the perpetrator is likely a teenager acting out of senseless destruction or revenge, a sentiment she believes the police share. She argues that such crimes are often the result of difficult circumstances, such as broken homes, and advocates for remedial education rather than just imprisonment.

Roya recounts the death of her husband, who was paralyzed by polio six years ago. He was killed in a traffic accident involving a stolen Volkswagen Beetle that had been taken from a man named Mr. Watkinson in Manchester. She maintains that the accident was manslaughter caused by irresponsible youth rather than premeditated murder. Poirot counters this by asserting that Joyce’s death was premeditated murder, as someone intentionally held her head underwater. He suggests the motive might be self-preservation, noting that Joyce had boasted shortly before her death that she had witnessed someone committing a killing.

Roya suggests Poirot consult Miss Emmeline, the headmistress, who she believes has psychological insight into the students. Poirot also inquires about an exchange student who served as a companion to Roya's aunt, Lady Llewellyn-Smith. Roya reveals that the girl, who was friendly with a lawyer in Manchester previously involved in a forgery case, disappeared after Lady Llewellyn-Smith's death. Roya suspects the girl forged a codicil to her aunt's will.

After the conversation, Poirot walks to Helpsley Cemetery, a modern, tidy cemetery located near a small, centuries-old church.
